title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Hong Kong booksellers are reportedly arrested over alleged sales of seditious publications
stories.summary:
Hong Kong booksellers are reportedly arrested over alleged sales of seditious publications. Hong Kong authorities have raided two bookstores and arrested five people on suspicion of selling allegedly seditious publications, local media reported Wednesday, in the latest step targeting independent booksellers.

BBC: Five arrested after Hong Kong police raid independent bookshops.

BBC reports that officials say they are suspected of selling "seditious" books which incited "hatred" against authorities.

links.headline: Five arrested after Hong Kong police raid independent bookshops
links.excerpt: Officials say they are suspected of selling "seditious" books which incited "hatred" against authorities.
links.imageAlt: Five arrested after Hong Kong police raid independent bookshops

stories.title: Top secrets come spilling out
stories.summary:
Top secrets come spilling out.

In 1917, British analysts deciphered a coded message the German foreign minister sent to one of his country's diplomats vowing to begin "unrestricted submarine warfare" and seeking to win over Mexico with a promise to "reconquer the lost territory in Texas, New Mexico and Arizona" if the US entered the world war.

When it became public, the Zimmerman Telegram caused a sensation, helping propel the US into the conflict against Germany.

When it became public, the Zimmerman Telegram caused a sensation, helping propel the US into the conflict against Germany.

stories.summary:

stories.title: France gives final approval to assisted-dying bill
stories.summary:
France gives final approval to assisted-dying bill. France’s National Assembly gave final approval Wednesday to a bill allowing adults with incurable illnesses to receive lethal medication, the culmination of years of debate over end-of-life care.

The lower house of parliament approved the measure in a 291-241 vote, after backing it in three previous readings, completing parliament’s work on the legislation announced by French President […]. Washington Times: France's National Assembly will give final approval to assisted-dying bill after years of debate.

stories.title: Hegseth announces ‘High-T’ military initiative for testosterone testing and treatment
stories.summary:
Hegseth announces ‘High-T’ military initiative for testosterone testing and treatment. The Pentagon will conduct testosterone deficiency screenings for active-duty service members who are at least 30 years old and recommend hormonal treatments to boost the U.S. military’s strength, War Secretary Pete Hegseth announced on Wednesday.

stories.title: 100 Million Americans Could Soon Face Smoky Skies Thanks To Canada
stories.summary:
100 Million Americans Could Soon Face Smoky Skies Thanks To Canada. Heavy smoke from Canadian wildfires is predicted to spread across the United States from Wednesday to Friday, causing potential air pollution that could impact 100 million people.

The hazy smoke from wildfires — still blazing in western Ontario and northern Minnesota on Tuesday — is forecast to drift toward the Midwest, Northeast, and Mid-Atlantic, according ... Washington Times: Busy wildfire season continues exposing millions in Midwest and Northeast U.S. to dangerous smoke.

Washington Times reports that heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is expected to engulf large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week, exposing millions of people to dangerous air pollution.

stories.title: Trump beats his own record for strategic backflips on Iran
stories.summary: Trump beats his own record for strategic backflips on Iran. Donald Trump's actions this week have only confirmed he has run out of options and is now engaged in a simple war of wills, and calculations on who will run out of munitions first, with an enemy that he has underestimated from the start.

stories.title: Australia has a love-hate relationship with cats – but under all the emotion is a fascinating history | Jodie Stewart
stories.summary:
Australia has a love-hate relationship with cats – but under all the emotion is a fascinating history | Jodie Stewart. We got our first feline hero in 1971 when Matthew Flinders’ heartfelt tribute to his pet Trim was discovered In 1872, the city of Melbourne hosted its first cat show one year after London’s Crystal Palace debuted its first show.

stories.title: Inside Ode with Anthropic, the startup betting AI services are the future of enterprise
stories.summary:
Inside Ode with Anthropic, the startup betting AI services are the future of enterprise. Can a handful of engineers really do the work of an army of consultants?

That’s the bet behind Ode with Anthropic — the joint venture dedicated to embedding forward-deployed engineers in enterprise firms, backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs and others.

On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Rebecca Bellan sits down with Ode’s leaders Chris Taylor and Eddie Siegel, who founded Fractional AI, […]. TechCrunch: Anthropic, Blackstone bet the next trillion-dollar AI business is implementation, not just models.

TechCrunch reports that anthropic-backed Ode launches as AI labs bet that embedding forward-deployed engineers inside enterprises is the key to accelerating enterprise AI adoption.

stories.title: Drivers charging electric cars handed shock parking fines
stories.summary:
Drivers charging electric cars handed shock parking fines. EV owners were sent hefty PCNs but say some signs in private car parks fail to warn of fees to park and recharge car Does refuelling your car class as parking?

The answer appears to be yes if it’s an electric vehicle.

Guardian Money has been contacted by several readers who were fined after charging their cars away from home. The motorists report being caught out by signs that fail to make clear that charging points are subject to parking tariffs or to store opening times.

stories.title: UK 16- and 17-year-olds to be encouraged to follow midnight social media curfew
stories.summary:
UK 16- and 17-year-olds to be encouraged to follow midnight social media curfew.

Midnight to 6am block on some apps is latest stage of Labour’s bid to protect young people from online harms Sixteen and 17-year-olds are to be encouraged to observe a midnight social media curfew, in the latest stage of Labour’s bid “to protect the next generation” from online harms, including poor sleep caused by night-time scrolling.

From next spring, Britain’s oldest children will be urged to refrain from using certain apps with a midnight to 6am block being switched on by def.

stories.summary:

Andrew Bailey warns that US will not be able to achieve its ambitions alone The Bank of England governor has called for international cooperation to tackle growing AI threats, warning that the US and Trump administration would not be able to achieve their ambitions alone.

stories.title: Apple quietly reveals how its Maps ads will differ from Google’s
stories.summary:
Apple quietly reveals how its Maps ads will differ from Google’s. Apple has published the policies governing its upcoming Maps advertising business, revealing a strategy that differs from Google’s.

The new rules prohibit home services businesses like plumbers, electricians, locksmiths, and roofers from advertising on Apple Maps, along with several other sensitive categories, suggesting Apple is taking a more curated approach to these ads.

links.headline: Apple quietly reveals how its Maps ads will differ from Google’s

stories.title: Fishers, residents seek DENR probe into Batangas cement factory
stories.summary:
Fishers, residents seek DENR probe into Batangas cement factory.

MANILA, Philippines — Fisherfolk and coastal residents in Lemery, Batangas, have launched a petition campaign calling on the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) to investigate the alleged environmental, health and livelihood impacts of a cement factory operating in Barangay Sinisian West.

The Pambansang Lakas ng Kilusang Mamamalakaya ng Pilipinas (Pamalakaya) announced the petition.

stories.title: Pia Cayetano: NBI’s SEA Games probe meant to ‘intimidate, distract us’
stories.summary:
Pia Cayetano: NBI’s SEA Games probe meant to ‘intimidate, distract us’. MANILA, Philippines — Sen. Pia Cayetano has accused National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Director Melvin Matibag of intimidation, distracting them from their duties as senator-judges by bringing up plans to investigate the alleged irregularities in the 2019 Southeast Asian (SEA) Games sports complex.

Her brother, Sen. Alan Peter Cayetano, was chairperson of the Philippine SEA Games. Philippine Daily Inquirer: Matibag: Probing 2019 SEA Games complex has nothing to do with Cayetano.

Philippine Daily Inquirer reports that mANILA, Philippines — National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Director Melvin Matibag on Wednesday said the bureau’s investigation into the alleged funding irregularities of a sports complex built in New Clark City, Tarlac which was used in the 2019 Southeast Asian (SEA) Games, is just “incidental.”

links.headline: Matibag: Probing 2019 SEA Games complex has nothing to do with Cayetano
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines — National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Director Melvin Matibag on Wednesday said the bureau’s investigation into the alleged funding irregularities of a sports complex built in New Clark City, Tarlac which was used in the 2019 Southeast Asian (SEA) Games, is just “incidental.” He added that it has nothing to do with Sen.
